profile_yaml:
# Profile configuration template — copy this directory to profiles/default/ and customise.
# This file controls pre-filtering keywords, LLM scoring settings, and output options.

# ── Pre-filter keywords ─────────────────────────────────────────────
keywords:
  # Title patterns (case-insensitive regex).
  # Split into tiers so you can be precise about your core targets
  # while still catching adjacent roles.
  title_patterns:
    high_confidence:
      - "\\bstaff\\s+engineer\\b"
      - "\\bsenior\\s+software\\s+engineer\\b"
      - "\\bsenior\\s+backend\\s+engineer\\b"
      - "\\bstaff\\s+software\\s+engineer\\b"
    broad:
      - "\\bsoftware\\s+engineer\\b"
      - "\\bbackend\\s+engineer\\b"
      - "\\bfull.?stack\\s+engineer\\b"
      - "\\bplatform\\s+engineer\\b"

  # Job description should contain at least N of these signals.
  description_signals:
    min_matches: 1
    patterns:
      - "\\b(python|typescript|go|rust|java)\\b"
      - "\\b(kubernetes|docker|aws|gcp|azure)\\b"
      - "\\b(postgresql|mysql|redis|kafka)\\b"
      - "\\b(ci/cd|github\\s+actions|jenkins)\\b"
      - "\\b(microservices|distributed\\s+systems)\\b"
      - "\\b(api|rest|grpc|graphql)\\b"

  # Hard exclusions — skip regardless of title match.
  exclusions:
    - "\\bintern\\b"
    - "\\bjunior\\b"
    - "\\bon-?site\\s+only\\b"

  # Skip jobs whose location string matches these.
  location_exclusions:
    - "\\b(us only|usa only|north america only|india|latam|australia|asia)\\b"

  # Jobs must mention at least one of these location strings to pass.
  location_patterns:
    - "\\b(europe|eu|uk|germany|netherlands|poland|spain|portugal)\\b"
    - "\\bemea\\b"

  # Fallback: jobs matching these but not location_patterns are still considered,
  # just assigned a lower tier.
  remote_patterns:
    - "\\b(remote|worldwide|global|distributed|anywhere|flexible)\\b"

  # Tier assigned when a job only matches remote_patterns, not location_patterns.
  fallback_tier: "signal_match"

# ── Scoring config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
scoring:
  model: "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"   # Haiku = lowest cost; change to sonnet for higher quality
  max_description_chars: 20000           # Truncate long descriptions before sending to LLM
  min_score_to_display: 50             # Hide jobs scoring below this threshold
  concurrency: 25
  batch_size: 5

# ── Output config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
output:
  terminal: true
  markdown_report: true
  telegram:
    enabled: false   # Set to true and add TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN + TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID to .env
    top_n: 5
profile_doc:
# Candidate Profile — Your Name

<!-- This document is sent verbatim to the LLM as your "CV" for job scoring.
     The more specific you are, the better the scoring quality.
     Replace all placeholder text with your real experience.
     Sections like "Critical Skill Gaps" and "What Lowers Fit" are especially
     important — they prevent the LLM from inflating scores on bad matches. -->

## Role Target
Senior / Staff Software Engineer — Backend or Platform Engineering
Open to: distributed systems, developer tooling, infrastructure-adjacent roles

## Experience Summary
- 8+ years of professional software engineering experience
- Current role: Senior Software Engineer at [Company] (remote), [start] — present
- Prior: [Title] at [Company], [Title] at [Company]
- Proven technical leadership: system design, cross-team collaboration, mentoring
- Industries: [e.g. fintech, SaaS, e-commerce, infrastructure]
- Education: M.Sc. / B.Sc. [Degree] — [University]

## Core Technical Stack

### Languages
- Python (primary)
- TypeScript / JavaScript
- Go (secondary)
- Bash

### Frameworks & Libraries
- FastAPI, Django
- Next.js, React
- pytest, Vitest

### Databases & Messaging
- PostgreSQL (primary)
- Redis
- Kafka (familiar)
- Elasticsearch (basic)

### Infrastructure & CI/CD
- AWS (EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda) — production experience
- Docker, Docker Compose
- GitHub Actions, GitLab CI
- No Kubernetes production experience (learning)

### Practices
- API design (REST, GraphQL)
- Microservices architecture
- System design and technical documentation
- Code review, mentoring junior engineers
- Agile / Scrum

## What Makes a Good Match (Score Higher)
- Backend-focused roles (Python or Go preferred)
- Platform / developer tooling / infrastructure engineering
- API-heavy roles with clear ownership
- Remote-first or remote-friendly (European timezones)
- Senior or Staff level (not principal/director, not junior/mid)
- Product companies over agencies/consultancies
- Roles with greenfield or significant technical ownership
- Companies with strong engineering culture and code review practices
- Fintech, SaaS, or developer-tools companies

## Critical Skill Gaps (Penalise Hard When Required)
These are confirmed gaps — not "learning quickly" adjacencies. When a job's
primary focus depends on these, lower tech_stack_match significantly:
- **Kubernetes / container orchestration**: No production Kubernetes experience
- **Mobile development**: No iOS or Android experience
- **ML / data engineering**: No production ML pipelines or data warehousing
- **C++ / Rust / systems programming**: No experience with low-level languages
- **Frontend as primary focus**: React is familiar but not a primary strength

## What Lowers Fit (Score Lower)
- On-site only positions
- US-only or Americas-only timezone requirements
- Junior or entry-level positions
- Staffing agency / body-shop contract roles
- Roles where the primary language is Java or C# (no recent experience)
- Roles requiring deep ML/data engineering as the sole focus
- Pure frontend roles

## Location & Work Setup
- Based in: [City], [Country]
- Work authorization: [e.g. EU citizen — no sponsorship needed]
- Preferred: Fully remote
- Acceptable: Remote with occasional travel, [timezone] overlap
- Not acceptable: On-site required

## Career Direction
Looking to grow into a Staff Engineer or technical lead role with broader system
design scope. Interested in roles where backend engineering intersects with
developer productivity or platform infrastructure. The ideal next move is a
Senior/Staff backend role at a strong product company, with scope to influence
architecture and mentor more junior engineers.

## Portfolio
- **[Project Name]** (github.com/yourhandle/project)
  - [Tech stack used]
  - [What it demonstrates — architecture, specific skills, etc.]
